title: Sprint 7: CI Health + NPT 20ps Validation Gate
problem_statement: Fix blocking CI failure in test_langevin_step_finite (safe_map reshape bug), investigate batched_equilibrate NaN, run oracle-mandated NPT 20ps stability gate, confirm EFA/MTT backlog status, and update CLAUDE.md for v1.0 release readiness.

steps.description: PARALLEL Day 1: Kick off NPT 20ps background run (40000 steps, 2-4 TIP3P waters, 300K, 1 bar, dt=0.5fs) AND recon safe_map reshape failure with compound pytrees (PaddedSystem + LangevinState)
steps.rationale: NPT run is longest-pole task; start immediately in parallel with CI work
steps.gate: NPT run started; safe_map bug confirmed

steps.description: Fix safe_map reshape bug at batched_simulate.py:593-631 — add assertion on uniform leading dim; test all call sites (batched_minimize, batched_equilibrate, batched_produce)
steps.rationale: CI blocker; localized fix
steps.gate: test_langevin_step_finite passes; all batched_ callers verified

steps.description: Add regression test for safe_map with heterogeneous-leaf pytree input (PaddedSystem + LangevinState compound)
steps.rationale: Prevent future safe_map regressions on compound pytrees
steps.gate: New test present and passing

steps.description: Collect NPT 20ps results; evaluate against tightened gates: T=300±5K mean (last 10ps), P mean=1±50 bar (last 10ps), density Δρ<2% of 0.985 g/cm³ (HARD gate), no NaN
steps.rationale: Oracle-mandated 20ps gate with tightened thresholds
steps.gate: All 4 gates pass OR fallback: mark NPT experimental if any gate fails

steps.description: Update CLAUDE.md: NPT production example with 20ps validation evidence, batched_equilibrate cold-start safe pattern (defer NaN trace to Sprint 8), v1.0 constraints summary
steps.rationale: Deferred from Sprint 6; needed for v1.0
steps.gate: CLAUDE.md complete and accurate

steps.description: Write CHANGELOG/release notes for v1.0: dt<=0.5fs constraint, NPT validated to 20ps, batched_equilibrate cold-start requirement, v2.0 roadmap pointer (constraint-aware thermostat)
steps.rationale: Users need release notes; CLAUDE.md is internal
steps.gate: CHANGELOG entry present

steps.description: [TIME-PERMITTING] Recon EFA production validation status; update .praxia/backlog.jsonl MTT entry with confirmed status for Sprint 8 planning
steps.rationale: Informs Sprint 8 MTT scheduling; not v1.0 blocker
steps.gate: EFA status documented in backlog

steps.description: Auditor: final v1.0 release readiness check across Sprint 7 changes
steps.rationale: Final gate before release tag
steps.gate: Audit PASS or specific blockers documented

conditions: Parallelize NPT 20ps run Day 1 with safe_map recon — do not serialize
Drop step 4 (oracle NaN scope decision); defer batched_equilibrate NaN to Sprint 8; document cold-start safe pattern in CLAUDE.md
Tighten NPT acceptance gates: mean P over last 10ps = 1±50 bar (not ±200); density Δρ <2% of 0.985 g/cm³ as HARD gate; T=300±5K mean over last 10ps
Add regression test for safe_map with heterogeneous-leaf pytree (PaddedSystem + LangevinState)
Add release notes / CHANGELOG entry: dt<=0.5fs constraint, 20ps NPT evidence, batched_equilibrate cold-start requirement, v2.0 roadmap pointer
EFA status recon (step 6) is time-permitting only; not v1.0 critical path
Optional: 1ps NPT smoke test on small protein-in-water; not a blocker
oracle_notes.approval_note: All 7 conditions addressed with one-to-one mapping. Tightened gates match specification. Step 8 auditor gate closes release loop cleanly.

title: Sprint 8: Batched test fixes + NPT 20ps gate + EFA sign-off
problem_statement: Three batched test call sites have API mismatches (test-side only): batched_minimize returns 3-tuple but test assigns to single var (line 174); batched_equilibrate requires (unique_batch, system_index, positions, key) but test calls (batch, key=..., n_steps=...) (line 186); batched_produce_streaming missing system_index arg (line 260). NPT 20ps run script has broken pressure calc at line 105. No 20ps validation test exists. EFA integrated but production readiness unconfirmed; blocks MTT backlog.

steps.description: Fix test_batched_minimize (line 174): unpack 3-tuple return: minimized_pos, converged, rms = batched_minimize(...)
steps.rationale: Test-only change; unblocks CI
steps.gate: test_batched_minimize passes

steps.description: Fix test_batched_equilibrate (line 186): add system_index=jnp.arange(B), pass system_index and batch.positions as positionals; remove invalid n_steps kwarg
steps.rationale: Test API call mismatch
steps.gate: test_batched_equilibrate passes

steps.description: Fix test_batched_produce_streaming (line 260): add system_index=jnp.arange(B) as 2nd positional arg
steps.rationale: Test API call mismatch
steps.gate: test_batched_produce_streaming passes

steps.description: Fix NPT pressure bug in /tmp/npt_20ps_run.py line 105. Current broken code: `P_inst = float(jnp.prod(state.box[0])) / volume`. Replace with: (1) compute virial W = stress.virial_trace(positions, forces) [kcal/mol/Å³·Å³], (2) compute KE from momenta and masses, (3) call pressure.instantaneous_pressure_akma(KE, W, volume), (4) convert to bar: P_bar = P_akma * units.BAR_PER_AKMA_PRESSURE (=69477.0, units.py:32). Also copy script to scripts/validation/npt_20ps_run.py for durability.
steps.rationale: Critical bug; broken pressure calc invalidates 20ps results. BAR_PER_AKMA_PRESSURE=69477.0 at src/prolix/physics/units.py:32. virial_trace at stress.py:14; instantaneous_pressure_akma at pressure.py:14.
steps.gate: Pressure calc matches test_npt_barostat pattern

steps.description: Run fixed NPT 20ps script (40000 steps, 16 TIP3P waters, 300K, 1 bar, dt=0.5fs). 16 waters provides physically defensible pressure statistics (4 waters too small — instantaneous P fluctuations ~thousands of bar). Save results to /tmp/npt_20ps_results_final.json with full (T, P, V, density) timeseries.
steps.rationale: Long-pole task; must complete before audit gate
steps.gate: No NaN; timeseries complete and finite

steps.description: Audit NPT 20ps results (16 waters) against oracle-mandated gates: (i) T_mean(last 10ps) = 300±5K [absolute Kelvin], (ii) P_mean(last 10ps) = 1±100 bar [absolute bar, verify via BAR_PER_AKMA_PRESSURE=69477 at units.py:32], (iii) density error = |computed - 0.985| / 0.985 < 2% [HARD gate], (iv) no NaN in full timeseries. P gate widened from ±50 to ±100 bar: physically defensible for 16-water box. All 4 must PASS for 'approved'; log explicit failure reason if any fail.
steps.rationale: Oracle-mandated gate
steps.gate: All 4 gates PASS or explicit FAIL reason

steps.description: Recon: audit EFA completeness at src/prolix/physics/efa_coulomb.py; verify EFAParams, _lebedev_26, _fit_efa_params, efa_coulomb_energy all present and jit-compilable
steps.rationale: Prerequisite scope for EFA validation
steps.gate: jit(efa_coulomb_energy) compiles without error

steps.description: Write EFA acceptance test (tests/physics/test_efa_coulomb.py, new). Verify: (i) accuracy: efa_coulomb_energy kernel output matches reference erf(αr)/r at r=[0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0] Å within documented tolerance, (ii) determinism: multiple calls produce identical output, (iii) no NaN/inf across [0.3, 10] Å range. NOTE: efa_coulomb_energy is NOT wired into any production energy path (confirmed by rg). EFA 'production-ready' for MTT purposes means the standalone kernel API is correct; MTT Log-Det Estimator depends on the kernel API only, not production wiring. Document this scope explicitly in the test file docstring.
steps.rationale: MTT unblock requires verified kernel API, not production-path wiring. efa_coulomb_energy not imported by any src/ module outside efa_coulomb.py (confirmed). MTT only needs standalone kernel correctness.
steps.gate: Test PASS (EFA ready) or FAIL with specific reason

steps.description: Add test_npt_20ps_stability_gate to tests/physics/test_npt_barostat.py (marked slow). n_waters=16, steps=40000, dt=0.5fs, T=300K, P=1bar. Assertions: T_mean(last 10ps) in [295, 305]K; P_mean(last 10ps) in [-100, 102] bar; density error < 2%; no NaN. Encode only after step 6 (audit) passes.
steps.rationale: Commits oracle gate to test suite
steps.gate: Test present and passes

steps.description: Oracle: final review of all three work streams (CI health, NPT stability, EFA readiness). Verdict: APPROVED or CONDITIONAL with explicit blockers
steps.rationale: Strategic sprint close gate
steps.gate: Oracle verdict issued

revisions_satisfied.R1_pressure_units: Step 4 cites BAR_PER_AKMA_PRESSURE=69477.0 at units.py:32 (verified); mandates virial_trace + instantaneous_pressure_akma; copies script to scripts/validation/ for durability.
revisions_satisfied.R2_water_count_and_gates: 16 TIP3P waters at Steps 5/6/9; P gate widened to ±100 bar with stated rationale; T ±5K and density <2% HARD gates preserved.
revisions_satisfied.R3_efa_scope: Step 8 explicitly scopes EFA as standalone kernel (not production-wired); MTT depends on kernel API only; scope documented in test docstring.

oracle_notes.minor_note: Step 9 P window encoded as [-100, 102] bar (asymmetric vs target 1±100); 1-bar discrepancy is below noise and non-blocking.
oracle_notes.approval_note: All three required corrections addressed cleanly. Cleared to execute.

outcome_narrative: 2/3 streams complete. EFA: 9/9 tests pass, production-ready, MTT unblocked. Batched API fixes: 3 call sites corrected (parse-level verified; end-to-end blocked by pre-existing flash_nonbonded.py:175 dtype bug). NPT 20ps gate: DEFERRED — gas-phase init + PME cutoff geometry requires >=200 waters and two-phase NVT->NPT protocol. Short NPT tests pass; integrator correct.
deferred_to_sprint9: NPT 20ps gate: two-phase NVT->NPT protocol with >=200 waters
lax.scan dtype carry audit across flash_*.py

title: Sprint 9: Float64 Compatibility + NPT 20ps Gate + MTT Theory Foundation
problem_statement: Three blocking issues prevent v1.0 progression: (1) flash_nonbonded.py has four clusters of jnp.float32() literals that force computation into float32, breaking float64 simulations; (2) NPT 20ps gate still lacks a validated two-phase NVT→NPT protocol at liquid water density; (3) MTT log-det estimator is unblocked but mtt_theory.md design doc is missing, blocking implementation.

steps.description: Fix float32 literal in Born radii padding sanitization at flash_nonbonded.py:187. Replace `jnp.float32(1.5)` with plain Python float `1.5` (auto-promotes to positions.dtype). Also scan lines 180-195 for any other float32 literals in chunked_born_radii.
steps.rationale: Surgical single-site fix. Fixer specializes in precision-aware code changes.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no matches in lines 180-195

steps.description: Fix float32 literals in soft-core + GB prefactor calculation at flash_nonbonded.py:231-236. Replace `jnp.float32(soft_core_lambda)`, `jnp.float32(0.5)`, `jnp.float32(-0.5)`, `jnp.float32(COULOMB_CONSTANT)`, `jnp.float32(tau)`, `jnp.float32(1e-8)` with plain Python literals. GB energy must accumulate in positions.dtype.
steps.rationale: HIGH severity: GB energy forced to float32 breaks physics fidelity. chunked_fused_energy is the core energy accumulator.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no matches in lines 225-245

steps.description: Fix float32 literals in _sparse_exclusion_energy at flash_nonbonded.py:366-368. Replace `jnp.float32(soft_core_lambda)` and `jnp.float32(0.5)` with plain Python floats.
steps.rationale: Exclusion correction computed in float32, then subtracted from float64 total — precision loss at each correction.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no matches in lines 360-375

steps.description: Fix float32 literals in parameter sanitization at flash_nonbonded.py:456-462 (flash_nonbonded_forces). Replace jnp.float32(1.5), jnp.float32(1.2), jnp.float32(0.0), jnp.float32(1.0) with plain Python floats. These sanitize padded atom parameters — forcing float32 here poisons all downstream energy computation.
steps.rationale: HIGH severity: sanitized parameters passed to energy_fn; float32 at this entry point propagates through entire nonbonded pipeline.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no matches in lines 450-470

steps.description: Write regression test tests/physics/test_flash_nonbonded_dtype.py. Test parametrized over [jnp.float32, jnp.float64]: (a) construct 8-atom water positions at target dtype, (b) call flash_nonbonded_forces, (c) assert output dtype == input dtype, (d) assert energy is finite. Also test that float32→float64 gives consistent energy values (within 1e-4 relative tolerance). Mark float64 tests as slow.
steps.rationale: Regression gate to prevent future float32 regressions. Written by fixer immediately after fixes.
steps.gate: pytest tests/physics/test_flash_nonbonded_dtype.py -v exits 0; all parametrized variants pass

steps.description: Run existing flash_nonbonded test suite after fixes: `pytest tests/physics/test_flash_nonbonded*.py -v`. Verify zero regressions from float32→plain-float replacement.
steps.rationale: Confirm fixes do not alter float32 behavior — plain Python floats auto-promote to float32 when array is float32.
steps.gate: pytest exit code 0; all previously-passing tests still pass

steps.description: Implement two-phase NVT→NPT protocol test at tests/physics/test_npt_barostat.py (add test_npt_20ps_liquid_water, marked slow): (1) _grid_water_positions(n_waters=64, spacing_angstrom=3.1) for liquid TIP3P density (~42 Å box), (2) settle.get_water_indices(0, 64), (3) NVT phase: settle_langevin init_fn+apply_fn for 1500 steps at dt=0.5fs, T=300K to relax grid bad contacts, (4) construct NPTState from NVT final state + box, (5) settle_csvr_npt for 40000 steps (20ps) at P=1 bar. Record (T, P, box_volume) every 200 steps.
steps.rationale: Complex integration test requiring careful state management across two integrators. General-purpose for multi-step JAX numerical code.
steps.gate: 40000 NPT steps complete without NaN/exception; timeseries arrays have shape (200, 3)

steps.description: Validate NPT 20ps trajectory thermodynamics: (a) T_mean over last 10ps (steps 20000-40000) in [295, 305] K, (b) P_mean over last 10ps in [-49, 51] bar, (c) no NaN in timeseries, (d) box volume varies smoothly (no sudden jumps >5%). Encode acceptance assertions directly in the test. Use rigid_tip3p_box_ke_kcal for temperature and pressure.instantaneous_pressure_akma for pressure (both confirmed exist from Sprint 8).
steps.rationale: Oracle-mandated thermodynamic gate. Auditor verifies assertions are physically correct and complete.
steps.gate: test_npt_20ps_liquid_water PASSES all 4 assertions; audit confirms assertions are correctly derived

steps.description: Write references/notes/mtt_theory.md algorithm design document covering: (1) Problem statement: stochastic log-det of Coulomb-weighted graph Laplacian, (2) Hutchinson trace estimator: E[z^T M^{-1} z] = tr(M^{-1}) for z~N(0,I), (3) Lanczos tridiagonalization: JAX lax.while_loop implementation sketch, (4) Chebyshev polynomial approximation of log via spectral density, (5) Connection to EFA kernel (efa_lebedev_coulomb_energy as kernel function), (6) Error bounds and convergence: expected K probes and M polynomial degree for <5% error on 256-atom box, (7) Dense eigendecomposition baseline for validation. This doc gates Step 10.
steps.rationale: Theory synthesis from algorithm design literature. General-purpose can research and draft algorithm documents.
steps.gate: File exists at references/notes/mtt_theory.md with all 7 sections present; Hutchinson and Lanczos pseudocode present

steps.description: Implement MTT log-det estimator skeleton at src/prolix/physics/mtt_logdet.py. Following mtt_theory.md design: (1) mtt_params = mtt_logdet_params(efa_params, n_probes, n_lanczos) [offline], (2) log_det_est = mtt_estimate_log_det(positions, charges, atom_mask, mtt_params) [online, JIT-able], (3) Lanczos via lax.while_loop, (4) Chebyshev approx of log eigenvalues, (5) Hutchinson averaging. Create tests/physics/test_mtt_logdet.py: JIT compiles; deterministic with same seed; <5% error vs numpy.linalg.slogdet on 8-atom test case.
steps.rationale: Greenfield JAX numerical implementation. General-purpose for novel algorithm code requiring iterative design.
steps.gate: test_mtt_logdet.py passes: JIT compile, determinism, <5% error on 8-atom Coulomb matrix

title: Sprint 9: Float64 Compatibility + NPT 20ps Gate + MTT Theory Foundation
problem_statement: Three blocking issues prevent v1.0 progression: (1) flash_nonbonded.py has four clusters of jnp.float32() literals that force computation into float32, breaking float64 simulations (lines 187, 231-236, 366, 456-462); (2) NPT 20ps gate still lacks a validated two-phase NVT→NPT protocol at liquid water density; (3) MTT log-det estimator is unblocked but mtt_theory.md design doc is missing, blocking implementation.

steps.description: Fix float32 literal in Born radii padding sanitization at flash_nonbonded.py:187. Replace `jnp.float32(1.5)` with plain Python float `1.5` (auto-promotes to positions.dtype). IMPORTANT: first grep for `dtype=jnp.float32` in the file — those are dtype arguments and must NOT be replaced; only bare `jnp.float32(<literal>)` calls should be changed. Also scan lines 180-195 for any other float32 literals.
steps.rationale: Surgical single-site fix. Fixer specializes in precision-aware code changes.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no bare jnp.float32(<number>) in lines 180-195; dtype= arguments preserved

steps.description: Fix float32 literals in soft-core + GB prefactor calculation at flash_nonbonded.py:231-236. Replace `jnp.float32(soft_core_lambda)`, `jnp.float32(0.5)`, `jnp.float32(-0.5)`, `jnp.float32(COULOMB_CONSTANT)`, `jnp.float32(tau)`, `jnp.float32(1e-8)` with plain Python literals. GB energy must accumulate in positions.dtype. Apply grep guard from Step 1: preserve any `dtype=jnp.float32` arguments.
steps.rationale: HIGH severity: GB energy forced to float32 breaks physics fidelity. chunked_fused_energy is the core energy accumulator.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no bare jnp.float32(<number>) in lines 225-245

steps.description: Fix float32 literals in _sparse_exclusion_energy at flash_nonbonded.py:366-368. Replace `jnp.float32(soft_core_lambda)` and `jnp.float32(0.5)` with plain Python floats. Apply grep guard.
steps.rationale: Exclusion correction computed in float32, subtracted from float64 total — precision loss at each correction.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no bare jnp.float32(<number>) in lines 360-375

steps.description: Fix float32 literals in parameter sanitization at flash_nonbonded.py:456-462 (flash_nonbonded_forces). Replace jnp.float32(1.5), jnp.float32(1.2), jnp.float32(0.0), jnp.float32(1.0) with plain Python floats. These sanitize padded atom parameters — forcing float32 here poisons all downstream energy computation. Apply grep guard.
steps.rationale: HIGH severity: sanitized parameters passed to energy_fn; float32 at this entry point propagates through entire nonbonded pipeline.
steps.gate: grep -n 'jnp.float32' src/prolix/physics/flash_nonbonded.py shows no bare jnp.float32(<number>) in lines 450-470

steps.description: Write regression test tests/physics/test_flash_nonbonded_dtype.py. Test parametrized over [jnp.float32, jnp.float64]: (a) construct 8-atom water positions at target dtype, (b) call flash_nonbonded_forces, (c) assert output dtype == input dtype, (d) assert energy is finite. Also test float32→float64 gives consistent energy values within 1e-4 relative tolerance. Mark float64 tests as slow.
steps.rationale: Regression gate to prevent future float32 regressions. Written by fixer immediately after fixes.
steps.gate: pytest tests/physics/test_flash_nonbonded_dtype.py -v exits 0; all parametrized variants pass

steps.description: Run existing flash_nonbonded test suite after fixes: `pytest tests/physics/test_flash_nonbonded*.py -v`. Verify zero regressions from float32→plain-float replacement.
steps.rationale: Confirm fixes do not alter float32 behavior — plain Python floats auto-promote to float32 when array is float32.
steps.gate: pytest exit code 0; all previously-passing tests still pass

steps.description: Implement two-phase NPT test at tests/physics/test_npt_barostat.py (add test_npt_20ps_liquid_water, marked slow): (1) _grid_water_positions(n_waters=64, spacing_angstrom=3.1) for liquid TIP3P density (~42 Å box), (2) settle.get_water_indices(0, 64), (3) NVT phase: settle_langevin init_fn+apply_fn for 4000 steps at dt=0.5fs (2 ps) to relax grid bad contacts — MUST be at least 4000 steps per oracle mandate, (4) construct NPTState from NVT final state + box, (5) settle_csvr_npt for 40000 steps (20ps) at P=1 bar, tau_thermostat=2000 AKMA, tau_barostat=2000 AKMA. Record (T, P, box_volume) every 200 steps.
steps.rationale: Complex integration test requiring careful state management across two integrators. 4000-step NVT ensures bad contacts from 3.1 Å grid spacing are relaxed before barostat engages.
steps.gate: 40000 NPT steps complete without NaN/exception; timeseries arrays have shape (200, 3)

steps.description: Validate NPT 20ps trajectory thermodynamics: (a) T_mean over last 10ps (steps 20000-40000) in [295, 305] K, (b) P_mean over last 10ps in [-99, 101] bar (±100 bar gate — consistent with Sprint 8 precedent for small explicit-water boxes; 64-water σ_P ~500-800 bar instantaneous, ~80-130 bar on 10ps mean), (c) no NaN in timeseries, (d) box volume varies smoothly — no sudden jumps >5%. Encode as pytest assertions in test_npt_20ps_liquid_water with docstring documenting ±100 bar rationale.
steps.rationale: Oracle-mandated thermodynamic gate. ±100 bar is physically justified for 64-water box (Sprint 8 precedent: 16 waters → ±100 bar). Auditor verifies assertions are correct.
steps.gate: test_npt_20ps_liquid_water PASSES all 4 assertions; P gate documented as ±100 bar with pressure fluctuation rationale in test docstring

steps.description: Write references/notes/mtt_theory.md algorithm design document covering all 7 sections: (1) Problem: stochastic log-det of Coulomb-weighted graph Laplacian L = D - K where K_ij = efa_lebedev_coulomb_energy kernel, (2) Hutchinson trace estimator: E[z^T M^{-1} z] = tr(M^{-1}) for z~N(0,I), (3) Lanczos tridiagonalization with JAX lax.while_loop implementation sketch — include re-orthogonalization strategy and stability note, (4) Chebyshev polynomial approximation of log via spectral density — include domain mapping for λ_min near zero (shift/regularization required), (5) Connection to EFA: efa_lebedev_coulomb_energy is the kernel function for K, reference efa_coulomb.py:209, (6) Error bounds: expected K (Hutchinson probes) and M (polynomial degree) for <5% error on 256-atom box — note N=256 is the milestone but Sprint 9 validates on N=64, (7) Dense eigendecomposition baseline (numpy.linalg.slogdet) for test validation.
steps.rationale: Theory synthesis must precede implementation. Sections 3 and 4 gate the Lanczos stability and Chebyshev domain choices in Step 10.
steps.gate: File exists at references/notes/mtt_theory.md with all 7 sections; sections 3 and 4 include explicit re-orthogonalization strategy and domain shift for near-zero eigenvalues

steps.description: Implement MTT log-det estimator at src/prolix/physics/mtt_logdet.py following mtt_theory.md design: (1) mtt_logdet_params(efa_params, n_probes, n_lanczos) -> MTTParams [offline], (2) mtt_estimate_log_det(positions, charges, atom_mask, mtt_params) -> scalar [online, JIT-able via functools.partial], (3) Lanczos via lax.while_loop with re-orthogonalization per theory doc, (4) Chebyshev approx of log eigenvalues with domain shift. Write tests/physics/test_mtt_logdet.py with three cases: (a) N=8 smoke test: JIT compiles, deterministic with same seed, (b) N=64 validation: <5% error vs numpy.linalg.slogdet on 64-atom Coulomb matrix [REQUIRED accuracy gate], (c) N=256 is the milestone target — explicitly defer to Sprint 10 with a skip marker in the test.
steps.rationale: Greenfield JAX numerical implementation. N=8 smoke + N=64 accuracy gate provides meaningful validation while deferring full 256-atom milestone.
steps.gate: pytest tests/physics/test_mtt_logdet.py passes N=8 smoke and N=64 <5% error; N=256 test present and marked skip with comment 'Sprint 10 milestone'

oracle_revision_notes: R1: NVT ≥4000 steps; R2: P gate ±100 bar; R3: N=64 MTT accuracy gate + skip N=256; R4: dtype= argument guard in float32 fix steps

title: Sprint 9: Float64 Compatibility + NPT 20ps Gate + MTT Theory Foundation

oracle_notes.R1: Satisfied. Step 7 specifies 4000 steps at dt=0.5fs (2 ps) with explicit 'MUST be at least 4000 steps' language.
oracle_notes.R2: Satisfied. Step 8 gate is [-99, 101] bar on 10ps mean with rationale documented.
oracle_notes.R3: Satisfied. Step 10 has N=64 REQUIRED accuracy gate and N=256 explicitly deferred to Sprint 10 with skip marker.
oracle_notes.R4: Satisfied. Steps 1-4 all include grep guard for dtype=jnp.float32 arguments.
oracle_notes.final_verdict: APPROVED. Sprint 9 plan cleared for implementation.

title: v1.1 Roadmap: closure→params refactor + prolix.export + batched_equilibrate fix + kUPS adapter
problem_statement: Sprint B closed (initialization artifact, not thermostat bug). Oracle approved v1.1 scope focused on two strategic goals: (1) seamless JAX pipeline integration, (2) StableHLO/WASM/WebGPU export. LFMiddle dropped (H1 falsified). Four work items approved in priority order.
strategic_goals: Seamless JAX pipeline integration for protein design/analysis
StableHLO -> WASM/WebGPU export for browser deployment
dropped_from_roadmap: LFMiddle (H1 falsified by Sprint B; no dt improvement expected)

steps.title: closure->explicit-params refactor
steps.description: Add make_energy_fn_pure(sys_dict) -> (params, fn) factory in system.py. fn(params, positions, box) is fully explicit and exportable. Keep existing closure API. Unit test: jax.export(fn) succeeds.

steps.title: Fix/retire batched_equilibrate NaN bug
steps.description: Trace NaN in batched_simulate.py::batched_equilibrate. If fixable in <1 day: fix + regression test. If structural: deprecate, remove from public API, promote cold-start pattern with docs. Update CLAUDE.md.

steps.title: kUPS convention adapter
steps.description: src/prolix/physics/kups_adapter.py with unit conversion (eV/A/amu <-> kcal/mol/A/AKMA). Update test_kups_thermostat_crossval.py to use adapter.

steps.title: prolix.export module (StableHLO)
steps.description: src/prolix/export.py with export_energy_fn, export_langevin_step, save/load helpers. Integration test: round-trip export->load->call == jit call. docs/EXPORT_GUIDE.md. Requires step 1.

deliverables: temperature tests updated to use pre-equilibrated box

summary: P2b cross-validation harness spec: 5 fixer tasks — t1 NVE energy conservation (settle_langevin guard), t2 NVT 216-water temperature stability, t3 NPT short cross-validation (10 ps, post b6e5bb9 fix), t4 ANALYTICAL vs AUTOGRAD per-term force agreement on MolecularBundle, t5 kUPS cross-val expanded to 3 system sizes (refactor + fix rng/key bug). All tests must use MolecularBundle not PhysicsSystem. Float64 required. Slow tests marked @pytest.mark.slow. Dependency order: t4 first (no infra deps), t1+t2 parallel (need NVT infra), t3 after t1+t2 (NPT blocked by same infra), t5 independent.

required_fixes: Create the spec file at the path named in the dispatch prompt: /home/marielle/projects/prolix/.praxia/docs/specs/260601_p2b-crossval-harness.md does not exist; the audited document is .praxia/docs/specs/260527_p2b-nonbonded-comparator.md

Clarify whether P2b scope is (a) only nonbonded vacuum-ala-dip harness as written in 260527 spec OR (b) the full cross-validation matrix from the roadmap (NVE, NVT 216-water, NPT, ANALYTICAL vs AUTOGRAD, kUPS 3-size sweep) — the spec and roadmap describe completely different scopes
Add explicit dependency note: f2 lists ExclusionSpec imported from prolix.physics.neighbor_list — confirm that symbol name is stable after P1a MolecularBundle refactor (bundles.py may have moved it)

Add the MolecularBundle vs PhysicsSystem boundary statement: the current spec targets PhysicsSystem (build_prolix_nonbonded_system returns PhysicsSystem); the roadmap Phase 2b targets MolecularBundle; the spec must explicitly state which API is targeted and why

Add NVE/NVT/NPT tasks: if P2b = full roadmap cross-validation matrix, f4–f5 energy/force tests cover only 2 of 6 required tests; NVE energy conservation, NVT 216-water temperature stability, NPT pressure+T, and ANALYTICAL vs AUTOGRAD parity tests are entirely absent from the spec
Add kUPS 3-system-size expansion: roadmap Phase 2b requires expanding kups_adapter.py tests to 100, 500, 2000 atoms — not mentioned anywhere in the spec

summary: 5-phase SETTLE-correctness sprint plan: NVE gamma=0 bisection of the residual rot-trans imbalance (decisive gate G-A), single-system fix on the selected branch, elevate the deferred batched-SETTLE vmap-vs-loop divergence to first-class, certify the 895-water gate, close. Reframed to protect Claim 1 and retire the red gate rather than lift the dt cap (explicit non-goal).
phases: Phase A NVE bisection diagnostic (decisive G-A gate)
Phase B single-system rot-trans fix (branch on G-A)
Phase C batched SETTLE divergence investigation (elevated)
Phase D certify 895-water gate + regression sweep + reviewer PASS
Phase E loose ends (reclassify 15374423, cleanup, CLAUDE.md, close)

decomposition.step: C1: Write scripts/explore/p5_substep_trot_trace.py — per-sub-step ΔT_rot/ΔT_trans trace on 64-water CPU box
decomposition.success_criteria: Script runs 500 steps locally, emits CSV with columns: step, substep, T_rot, T_trans, delta_T_rot, delta_T_trans. Sub-step drain site visible in cumulative delta.

decomposition.step: Based on C1 output: apply targeted fix — primary hypothesis is R-step dp missing AM correction (analogous to B2 for position SETTLE)
decomposition.success_criteria: T_rot moves toward 295-305 K range in local smoke test or cluster gate

decomposition.step: Cluster gate: 895-water NVT gate, campaign-tagged, SLURM submission

decomposition.success_criteria: lower succeeds, no custom_call in HLO
